hover_line: A bare room. Light on. Nothing moving.
vignette_text:
Just a room.
Table, chair, laptop.
Yesterday someone paced here, talking too loud on the phone.
Today it’s resting.
Guess even empty rooms get days off.

outcome_a_text.numb:
I count to three.
Nothing changes.
Good.
outcome_a_text.grounded:
I wait a moment.
Still empty.
Fair enough.
outcome_a_text.raw:
I wait anyway.
Like patience will fix me.
It won’t.

outcome_b_text.grounded:
Nothing’s happening here.
Can’t blame the room.
Onward.
outcome_b_text.raw:
I’m already leaving.
I don’t even give it a chance.
Of course I don’t.

reflection_a_text.numb:
It’s just a room.
Nothing to catch, nothing to miss.
reflection_a_text.grounded:
The light doesn’t change.
Nothing to catch, nothing to miss.
Just a room being a room.
reflection_a_text.raw:
Empty is easy.
Empty can’t reject me.
That’s why I’m staring.

reflection_b_text.grounded:
I’m not here to admire furniture.
There are better windows than this.
At least there used to be.
reflection_b_text.raw:
I say I’m picky.
Really I’m afraid.
So I keep moving.

post_hover_line: Still empty. The light hasn’t moved.

hover_line: Blue light flickers behind half-drawn curtains.
vignette_text:
A TV’s on somewhere inside.
Laugh track, canned and late.
Someone’s eating straight from the pan.
I can’t see them, but I know the rhythm.
Evenings sound the same when you stop listening.

outcome_a_text.numb:
Same track.
Same laughs.
I let it run.
outcome_a_text.grounded:
I let it play out.
Same timing, same voices.
Some things don’t surprise me anymore.
outcome_a_text.raw:
I keep listening.
Like I’m waiting for it to turn into my life.
It won’t.

outcome_b_text.numb:
Noise is noise.
I shut it off in my head.
outcome_b_text.grounded:
I stop listening halfway through.
I get the idea.
That’s usually enough.
outcome_b_text.raw:
I flinch away.
I don’t want warmth that isn’t mine.
So I pretend it’s pointless.

reflection_a_text.numb:
Background sound.
That’s all it is.
reflection_a_text.grounded:
Evenings sound the same when you stop listening.
A screen talking for people who won’t.
reflection_a_text.raw:
I used to need this.
A voice that filled the room.
So nobody had to.

reflection_b_text.grounded:
The laughter leaks out anyway.
Same timing, different room.
It’s just something to fill air.
reflection_b_text.raw:
I call it filler.
But it’s what I used to hide behind.
Still is.

memory_fragment_text.numb:
A table. Plates. Silence.
Something talking in the background.
memory_fragment_text.grounded:
Dinners were quiet after the shouting stopped.
The TV filled the space better than we could.
Nobody asked questions when something else was talking.
memory_fragment_text.raw:
We ate with the TV on because we couldn’t stand each other’s voices.
I learned to let screens speak for us.

post_hover_line: The TV’s still on. Someone changed the channel.

hover_line: A child leans over the sill, someone older nearby.
vignette_text:
The kid’s asking questions.
Too many, too fast. An older voice answers without hurrying.
No shouting, no edge to it.
Just time being spent.

outcome_a_text.numb:
I listen.
It’s harmless.
I move on.
outcome_a_text.grounded:
I stay longer than planned.
Doesn’t feel intrusive.
Just quiet.
outcome_a_text.raw:
I stay.
Because I want that tone aimed at me.
It hurts that it isn’t.

outcome_b_text.numb:
Not my problem.
Not my life.
outcome_b_text.grounded:
That’s their evening.
No need to hover.
They’re doing fine.
outcome_b_text.raw:
I leave fast.
If I listen too long, I start wanting.
And wanting makes me stupid.

reflection_a_text.grounded:
It’s calm.
Questions answered without teeth.
Time being spent.
reflection_a_text.raw:
I can’t stop measuring it against my own childhood.
That’s the part I pretend I’m not doing.

reflection_b_text.grounded:
It’s strange how calm it sounds.
Like nothing is about to break.
I forget people can have that.
reflection_b_text.raw:
I call it ‘calm’ like it’s a weather report.
It’s envy.
It’s grief.

memory_fragment_text.numb:
A voice. Slow. Patient.
A feeling I can’t name.
memory_fragment_text.grounded:
Someone used to answer me like that.
Slow, patient, never annoyed.
I don’t remember their face clearly.
Just how safe it felt to ask.
memory_fragment_text.raw:
I remember being safe for a while.
And then I remember it ending.
That’s what I can’t look at.

post_hover_line: The window’s still open. The voices moved away.

hover_line: Curtains drawn tight. No light leaks through.
vignette_text:
Can’t see anything inside.
Just fabric and a bit of movement.
No sound, either.
Someone’s home, probably.
Or maybe not.

outcome_a_text.numb:
I invent something.
It passes the time.
outcome_a_text.grounded:
My brain starts anyway.
It always does.
Even without clues.
outcome_a_text.raw:
I fill it in.
Because emptiness feels like threat.
Because I’m trained for it.

outcome_b_text.grounded:
Not everything needs a story.
This can stay closed.
outcome_b_text.raw:
I try to leave it blank.
But my mind doesn’t obey.
It never has.

reflection_a_text.numb:
Guessing is easy.
Reality is work.
reflection_a_text.grounded:
I can’t see inside, so I narrate it anyway.
A habit with sharp edges.
reflection_a_text.raw:
I make up danger even when nothing is happening.
That’s not imagination.
That’s damage.

reflection_b_text.grounded:
It’s quieter when I don’t guess.
No versions, no endings.
Just a window I don’t know.
reflection_b_text.raw:
Quiet is relief.
Relief feels suspicious.
So I keep scanning.

memory_fragment_text.numb:
Closed doors.
My mind doing laps.
memory_fragment_text.grounded:
I used to imagine what was happening in other rooms.
It felt safer than checking.
Sometimes I was right.
That never made it better.
memory_fragment_text.raw:
I rehearsed bad outcomes because being surprised was worse.
I called it ‘preparing.’
It was fear.

post_hover_line: The curtains don’t move. Still nothing to see.

hover_line: Two figures move around the same small kitchen.
vignette_text:
They’re cooking together.
Not talking much.
Someone reaches for the same drawer twice.
An apology, automatic.
They keep going.

outcome_a_text.numb:
Routine.
Two people moving around it.
Nothing more.
outcome_a_text.grounded:
This feels familiar enough.
No sparks, no disasters.
Just effort.
outcome_a_text.raw:
I watch too closely.
Like I’m hunting for the crack.
Like I need proof it can fail.

outcome_b_text.grounded:
Looks stable from here.
That’s usually enough.
outcome_b_text.raw:
I blur it on purpose.
If I see it clearly, I start wishing.
I don’t want to wish.

reflection_a_text.numb:
Two bodies.
One kitchen.
That’s it.
reflection_a_text.grounded:
Small habits.
Automatic apologies.
A quiet that isn’t sharp.
reflection_a_text.raw:
I keep checking whether they’re safe.
That’s not care.
That’s history.

reflection_b_text.grounded:
It’s quiet without being tense.
Everyone knows their role.
Nothing spills over.
reflection_b_text.raw:
I tell myself it’s ‘fine.’
What I mean is: it isn’t mine.
So I shouldn’t look.

memory_fragment_text.numb:
A kitchen.
Careful steps.
A feeling kept small.
memory_fragment_text.grounded:
I was in something like this once.
Not unhappy, just careful.
I didn’t give more than I had.
No one asked me to stay.
memory_fragment_text.raw:
I learned to be easy to live with.
Quiet. Useful.
And still disposable.

post_hover_line: The light’s still on. They’re eating now.

vignette_text:
Lights go out one by one.
A few stay on, doing nothing in particular.
Traffic thins. Voices don’t carry as far.
The building looks flatter without people in it.
Like it’s done for the night.

outcome_a_text.grounded:
Might as well enjoy the quiet.
It doesn’t last long.
outcome_a_text.raw:
I stay because leaving feels like ending.
And endings scare me more than silence.

outcome_b_text.grounded:
That’s enough for today.
No need to drag it out.
outcome_b_text.raw:
I cut it short.
Not because it’s enough.
Because I’m shaking.

reflection_a_text.numb:
Nothing else is going to happen.
Good.
reflection_a_text.grounded:
Nothing else is going to happen tonight.
The city’s already halfway asleep.
I’m not missing much.
reflection_a_text.raw:
I keep waiting for something to drop.
Even when the night is gentle.
Especially then.

reflection_b_text.grounded:
The day doesn’t need a closing scene.
It can just stop here.
That works too.
reflection_b_text.raw:
I don’t know how to stop softly.
So I stop abruptly.
Like slamming a door.

post_hover_line: Just dark windows. The night’s settled.

hover_line: A light clicks on. Someone moves closer to the glass.
vignette_text:
I’m not the only one looking.
They stop for a second, adjusting their eyes.
We notice each other at about the same time.
No rush. No anger.
Just an unexpected overlap.

outcome_a_text.grounded:
Well.
Guess that’s me, then.
Standing here.
outcome_a_text.raw:
I don’t look away.
I let it land.
I let myself be seen.

outcome_b_text.grounded:
Right.
Too much reality for one balcony.
Done.
outcome_b_text.raw:
I snap away.
Like being noticed is dangerous.
Because it used to be.

reflection_a_text.grounded:
They’ll forget me in ten seconds.
I’ll forget their face in five.
That’s fair.
reflection_a_text.raw:
I pretend it’s fair.
It’s not fairness.
It’s loneliness with rules.

reflection_b_text.grounded:
I break it first.
A habit that feels like control.
reflection_b_text.raw:
I leave before they can decide what I am.
That’s the reflex.
That’s the wound.

memory_fragment_text.numb:
Leaving early.
Keeping it simple.
Not being asked.
memory_fragment_text.grounded:
I learned early not to stay too long.
Leaving quietly kept things simple.
Most of the time, no one asked where I went.
And I didn’t volunteer it.
memory_fragment_text.raw:
I disappeared because being present made me a target.
Now I do it even when no one’s aiming.

post_hover_line: The window’s dark again. Nothing follows.

hover_line: Raised voices slip through an open window.
vignette_text:
Two people, same room.
Same volume, different words.
One of them laughs at the wrong time.
The other doesn’t like that.
It keeps circling back.

outcome_a_text.numb:
Same loop.
Same volume.
I watch.
outcome_a_text.grounded:
I let it run for a bit.
Same loop, same energy.
Predictable.
outcome_a_text.raw:
I stay with it.
Because it matches my body’s memory.
Because it feels like home.

outcome_b_text.grounded:
I cut it off mid-sentence.
Not my show.
Not tonight.
outcome_b_text.raw:
I can’t take it.
My chest tightens like it’s happening here.
So I run.

reflection_a_text.grounded:
They’ll keep going with or without me.
People always do.
I don’t need the whole track.
reflection_a_text.raw:
I know this rhythm.
I learned it before I learned sleep.
I still hear it in quiet rooms.

reflection_b_text.grounded:
Tuning out is a skill.
I got good at it early.
reflection_b_text.raw:
I tell myself I’m choosing peace.
What I’m choosing is avoidance.
And it costs.

memory_fragment_text.numb:
Shouting through walls.
Me turning it into weather.
memory_fragment_text.grounded:
I used to do this with real shouting.
Turn it into background, pretend it’s normal.
It worked in the moment.
It still shows up anyway.
memory_fragment_text.raw:
I trained myself not to react.
Now I don’t know how to stop reacting.
That’s what it did.

post_hover_line: The window’s still open. They lowered their voices.
